1.premise: A pre-lowered mic stand could be reframed as rare accommodation for short people.

2.premise: Backhanded celebrity comparisons feel like upgrades when the previous comparison was worse.

1.premise: A haircut is so ambiguous it implies two different decades of age simultaneously.

2.premise: A short man is like a Wish.com Jason Momoa because both are diminished versions of the same template.

3.premise: "Walmart Mark Wahlberg" could be reframed as simply being Donnie Wahlberg.
Donnie Wahlberg is unbeatable for reasons other than talent.

1.premise: "Can never beat Donnie" implies superior talent, but reveals he can barely perform.

2.premise: Marrying an anti-autism crusader would make sex self-defeating if the partner is autistic.

lines.text: Oh, my size microphone.

lines.text: Ah, it's a big deal for me.
lines.label: tag

lines.text: I get haircuts for this.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: Got a lot of different reactions on the haircut, though.

lines.label: setup

lines.text: "Melee, you look like you're in your 20s
lines.label: setup

lines.text: and 40s at the same time."

lines.text: Had a little girl run up to me, said,
lines.label: setup

lines.text: "You look like Jason Momoa, the Wish.com version."

lines.text: Heckler told me, "I look like Jason Momoa
lines.label: setup

lines.text: if he was Aquaman who only lived in a puddle.

lines.label: setup

lines.text: Which I'm fairly certain is just Donnie Wahlberg.

lines.text: But I can never beat Donnie.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: He can barely do that.

lines.text: It's 'cause he married Jenny McCarthy.

lines.label: setup
